steps.title: Apply similarity to identify equal angles
steps.content: Triangle $CAE \sim$ triangle $CBD$, with the vertex correspondence $C\leftrightarrow C$, $A\leftrightarrow B$, $E\leftrightarrow D$. Corresponding angles in similar triangles are congruent, so angle $CAE$ = angle $CBD$. The side scale factor ($AE = 26 \cdot BD$) does not affect angle measures.

steps.title: Find the requested value
steps.content:
Since angle $CBD = 57°$:
$$\angle CAE = \angle CBD = 57°$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
